steps.name: Implement Story.getVariable and InkEngine.getVariable
steps.description: Replace the SCAFFOLD stub for getVariable in Story.swift with real delegation to a new InkEngine.getVariable accessor. The accessor reads state.variablesState[name] and bridges the InkValue to a Swift native type (Int, Double, String, Bool) or nil. Walking skeleton: Story(json:) → getVariable('score') → returns 0 as Int.
acceptance_criteria: initial integer variable value is readable via getVariable
integer variable reads as Int
boolean variable reads as Bool
string variable reads as String
integer variable reads updated value after engine sets it via score_setup
boolean variable is true after story logic sets it during execution
unknown variable name returns nil without throwing
getVariable compiles with the correct public signature
steps.scenario_name: Walking Skeleton — getVariable initial value + US-01 — getVariable
quality_gates: All 8 US-01 acceptance tests pass GREEN
implementation_notes: Scaffold stub: public func getVariable(_ name: String) -> Any? { nil } — replace with real engine delegation
Add internal func getVariable(_ name: String) -> Any? to InkEngine — reads state.variablesState[name]
Bridge InkValue to Any?: .int(n) → n, .float(f) → Double(f), .string(s) → s, .bool(b) → b, .variablePointer → nil, missing key → nil
InkValue must NOT appear in the public signature (constraint D-03)
variablesState is initialized with global VAR defaults after Story(json:) — check InkDecoder for where initializeGlobalVariables is called
score_setup knot: ~ score = 42 — sets variablesState['score'] to .int(42); reward_check: ~ badge_awarded = true if score >= 10

steps.name: Implement Story.setVariable and InkEngine.setVariable
steps.description: Replace the SCAFFOLD stub for setVariable in Story.swift with real delegation to a new InkEngine.setVariable accessor. The accessor bridges the Swift value to InkValue and writes to state.variablesState[name] — only if the key already exists (pure no-op for unknown names).
acceptance_criteria: setVariable for Int is readable back via getVariable
setVariable for Bool is readable back via getVariable
setVariable for String is readable back via getVariable
setVariable with score 10 causes reward_check to output gold badge line
setVariable with has_key true causes locked_door to output door opens line
setVariable for player_name personalises start knot output
setVariable for unknown variable does not throw
setVariable does not change canContinue or currentChoices
quality_gates: All 8 US-02 acceptance tests pass GREEN
implementation_notes: Scaffold stub: public func setVariable(_ name: String, to value: some Any) {} — replace with real engine delegation
Add internal func setVariable(_ name: String, to value: some Any) to InkEngine
Pure no-op if state.variablesState[name] == nil — do NOT create new keys
Bridge: Int → .int, Double/Float → .float, String → .string, Bool → .bool, other → no-op
IMPORTANT: Test setVariable with Bool true for has_key → locked_door outputs 'The door swings open.' — verify fixture has this knot
The fixture slice-story-testability.ink.json must have: locked_door knot with { has_key: The door swings open. - else: You need a key. }
Inspect the fixture JSON to verify the knot names and variable names match exactly

steps.name: Implement Story.visitCount, InkEngine.visitCount/setVisitCount, and StoryTestSupport.setVisitCount
steps.description: Replace the SCAFFOLD stubs for visitCount in Story.swift (returns 0) and setVisitCount in StoryTestSupport.swift (no-op). Add InkEngine.visitCount and InkEngine.setVisitCount internal accessors. Story.visitCount reads state.visitCounts[name] ?? 0. setVisitCount in StoryTestSupport delegates to the engine accessor. Unknown name: no-op for set, 0 for get.
acceptance_criteria: visitCount returns 0 for unvisited knot
visitCount returns 1 after natural execution of prologue knot
setVisitCount write is readable back via visitCount
setVisitCount to 2 causes greeting to output welcome back line
greeting shows hello stranger when prologue visit count is 0
visitCount for unknown knot returns 0 without throwing
setVisitCount for unknown knot does not throw
quality_gates: All 7 US-03 acceptance tests pass GREEN
All US-01 and US-02 tests remain GREEN
implementation_notes: Story.visitCount(forKnot:) — replace stub return 0 with: engine.visitCount(forKnot: name)
Add InkEngine.visitCount(forKnot name: String) -> Int: return state.visitCounts[name] ?? 0
Add InkEngine.setVisitCount(forKnot name: String, to count: Int): guard state.visitCounts[name] != nil else { return }; state.visitCounts[name] = count
StoryTestSupport.swift: replace stub no-op with: engine.setVisitCount(forKnot: name, to: count) — uses @testable import for InkEngine access
IMPORTANT: verify visitCounts key format — inklecate uses dotted paths like 'prologue' or 'story.prologue'; inspect slice-story-testability.ink.json to confirm key format
Package.swift SwiftInkRuntimeTestSupport target is already declared — no Package.swift changes needed
setVisitCount unknown name: guard state.visitCounts[name] != nil — do NOT create new keys

steps.name: Implement Story.continueMaximally
steps.description: Replace the SCAFFOLD stub for continueMaximally in Story.swift with a real implementation: a while-loop over self.continue() that accumulates output strings. The loop exits when canContinue is false (either story ended or choice point reached). Decorated with @discardableResult.
acceptance_criteria: continueMaximally collects all lines from multi_line knot
continueMaximally output equals manual while-canContinue loop output
continueMaximally stops at choice point and currentChoices is non-empty
continueMaximally on ended story returns empty string
continueMaximally return value may be discarded without compiler warning
canContinue is false after continueMaximally drains terminal knot
quality_gates: All 6 US-04 acceptance tests pass GREEN
All US-01, US-02, US-03 tests remain GREEN
implementation_notes: Scaffold stub: @discardableResult public func continueMaximally() -> String { "" } — replace with real loop
Implementation is ENTIRELY facade-only: var output = ""; while canContinue { output += self.continue() }; return output
No InkEngine changes required for this step
The @discardableResult attribute is already on the scaffold — keep it
Verify the fixture has multi_line knot with three output lines, and with_choices knot with at least one choice
